program.name: Xero grad program 2026
program.duration_text: Our 12-month graduate program is designed to launch your career in either engineering, security, analysis, security engineering, or data science.
program.rotation_text: Technical grads complete three 4-month rotations over 12 months, working with teams across the business in their chosen field.

eligibility.work_rights: Either have the right to work full-time in Australia or will be eligible to work full-time after graduating.
minimum_requirements: Fewer than 12 months professional working experience in relevant industries.
Be able to start full-time in February 2026.
If you’re a current or past university student applying for a role in your field of study, you’ll need to provide a copy of your transcript.
You’ll also need to send your CV but only so we can use it to verify your eligibility.

stages.name: Logical and skills assessments
stages.details: Complete a logical reasoning assessment (~15 mins) and, if you’re applying for the technical program, a technical skills assessment (~60 mins). For technical roles, you’ll complete a HackerRank test, which you can practice beforehand.

stages.name: Record a short video interview
stages.details: Pre-record a video interview. If successful, you’ll be invited to attend a grad day.

stages.name: Virtual grad day
stages.details: A 1.5 hour virtual event assessed in two activities: a behavioural interview (30 min) and a technical assessment (30 min), plus a 15 minute introduction and a five minute break between sessions.

tips: Early applications aren’t given priority (apply any time before the closing date).
Don’t miss the closing date (applications past the close date aren’t accepted).
The entire Xero grad recruitment process is online, with a virtual assessment centre as the last stage, so you won’t need to travel.
For the technical skills assessment: HackerRank test which you can practice beforehand.

exec_themes: >4.6m global subscribers; building momentum and scaling the platform globally
Product expansion beyond core accounting (e.g., payroll, expenses, projects, ecosystem integrations)
AI-enabled productivity and automation (e.g., positioning of JAX as an in-product AI ‘financial superagent’)
Payments/bill-pay and broader financial services adjacency (implied via product features like ‘Pay bills’, ‘Accept payments’, and investor focus on Melio)
ESG & sustainability as a strategic commitment (net-zero roadmap; carbon accounting enablement for customers/advisors)
commercial_context.profit_engine: Subscription-based SaaS revenue from small business accounting platform (pricing plans) with monetization via tiered plans and adjacent products (e.g., payroll/expenses/projects) and ecosystem/embedded financial services such as payments and bill pay (including strategic focus on Melio).
commercial_context.headwinds: Macroeconomic pressure on small businesses (e.g., cash-flow stress) that may affect customer affordability/retention and purchasing of add-ons.
commercial_context.esg: Xero states sustainability is part of its approach and has published a Sustainability Report 2025; it also highlights climate targets/net-zero roadmap and partnerships to support carbon accounting and climate reporting (e.g., Sumday).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Increased emphasis on AI-driven experiences (JAX) and deeper push into payments/bill-pay via the Melio acquisition/investor briefings (2025–2026).

notes: The AU graduate overview page indicates the program kicks off in February 2026 but does not explicitly state application open/close dates.
The early careers page lists global locations and disciplines for the 12-month grad program but does not provide a single named cohort title or salary details.

notes: Investor PDFs (interim report, investor presentation, ASX releases) are linked from Xero’s investor site but were not retrievable via the provided tooling (brandfolder fetch returned an error), so themes/profit engine/headwinds are inferred from Xero’s official site navigation/content and investor-page context rather than quoted from formal reports.

‘Profit_engine’ and ‘recent_pivot’ are based on explicit product/pricing positioning and investor-page emphasis on Melio and AI (JAX), but detailed financial drivers (ARPU, churn, geographic mix, margins) are not available without the official reports.
‘Headwinds’ are supported by Xero research/media release about small-business cash-flow/economic challenges; this is not a financial risk disclosure section from an annual report.
